---
title: "data celaning"
author: '520004915'
date: "2024-03-30"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(GEOquery)
library(limma)
library(caret)
gse <- getGEO('GSE21374')# all samples are AR (rejection)
gse <- gse$GSE21374_series_matrix.txt.gz

feature<-fData(gse)
eMat<-exprs(gse)
eMat_df <- as.data.frame(eMat)
phe <- pData(phenoData(gse))

#---Data cleaning
missing_summary <- apply(feature,2,function(x) sum(x %in% ''))
#===
missing_symbol<-feature  %>% 
  select ('ID','Gene Symbol')%>%
  filter(`Gene Symbol` == '', !is.na(`Gene Symbol`))
proportion_of_missing_symbol <- nrow(missing_symbol)/nrow(feature)

## drop all [missing genes]
feature_new <-feature %>% filter(`Gene Symbol` != '', !is.na(`Gene Symbol`))
feature_new <- na.omit(feature_new)
## keep one gene symbol
#### I found there are many IDs is corresponding to more than 1 gene symbols, to formmat the data I will keep the first gene symbol
feature_new$'Gene Symbol' <- sapply(strsplit(feature_new$'Gene Symbol', ' /// '), function(x) x[1])
##after dropping rows from features, to keep the consistency, we also need to drop the corresponding rows in eMat
eMat_new <- eMat_df[rownames(eMat_df) %in% feature_new$ID, ]

##[Outliers]For eMat_new, the gene expression data frame, we need to keep those highly expressed genes
gene_means <- rowMeans(eMat_new)
gene_percentile <- rank(gene_means) / length(gene_means)
threshold <- 0.25 # we wanna keep 75% of the genes by ranking
filtered_genes <- eMat_new[gene_percentile > threshold,]

  ###**** you can add your visualisation of genes here (same as lab, do a boxplot)
## [normalisation]
exprSet <- normalizeBetweenArrays(filtered_genes)# normalisation
  ###*** you can add your visualisation here (boxplot, same as above) and we can compare 2 boxplots
### if the differences between max and min are within 50, it means we do not need log2 transform
qx <- as.numeric(quantile(exprSet,c(0.,0.25,0.5,0.75,0.99,1.0),na.rm=T))
logC <- (qx[5]>100) ||
           (qx[6]-qx[1]>50 && qx[2]>0) ||
            (qx[2]>0 && qx[2]<1 && qx[4]>1 && qx[4]<2)
if (logC){
  exprSet [which(exprSet <=0)] <- NaN
  exprSet_clean <- log2(exprSet)
  print('log2 transformation finished')
}else{
  print('does not need log2 transformation')
}


###[Cleaning Done]
expression <- as.data.frame(exprSet)
expression

```
